mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held above 5% represents restrictive monetary policy that raises the discount rate on future equity cash flows, producing a persistent downward drag on S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days regardless of other conditions.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and 20d forward returns is not negative and statistically significant (p>0.05) over any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE of more than 0.3 percentage points over three months signals labor market deterioration that precedes broader earnings weakness and recession risk, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: SPX 60d forward returns are positive or non-negative in more than 55% of historical instances where the UNRATE trigger fires.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS exceeds CPIAUCSL-implied inflation trend such that real policy rates are strongly positive, tightening real financial conditions jointly with elevated nominal rates compound to suppress equity valuations more than either series alone, producing negative 20-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS minus trailing 12m CPIAUCSL inflation rate > 2pp
failure_condition: Interaction term (real rate proxy) shows no incremental predictive power over FEDFUNDS alone as measured by Rank IC improvement < 0.02 across walk-forward folds.

mechanism_narrative: During a housing slowdown regime, a decline in HOUST below prior 12-month average signals weakening interest-rate-sensitive demand that historically leads broader economic contraction, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter; the same decline in an early-cycle recovery regime instead reflects healthy normalization and does not predict negative returns.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with restrictive monetary policy
failure_condition: Sign of the HOUST-return relationship is unstable (flips sign) across regimes without corresponding regime label, or Rank IC < 0.03 in late-cycle subsample.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in PAYEMS reflects real-time labor demand strength that feeds directly into consumer income and corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following week whenever payroll growth is positive.
failure_condition: Correlation between monthly PAYEMS growth sign and subsequent 5d SPX return sign is not significantly different from 50% (binomial test p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in INDPRO of more than 0.5% month-over-month signals contracting industrial output that precedes downward earnings revisions for cyclical sectors, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: 20d forward SPX returns following triggered INDPRO declines are positive in more than 55% of historical occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of rising UNRATE and decelerating PAYEMS growth indicates a genuine labor market turning point rather than noise in either series individually, and this combined slack signal predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter more reliably than either series alone.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rising for 2+ consecutive months AND PAYEMS monthly growth below trailing 6-month average
regime_hint: labor market turning point at late-cycle peak
failure_condition: Combined UNRATE-PAYEMS trigger shows no improvement in Rank IC versus UNRATE alone (delta < 0.02) across walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: In a low-and-stable-inflation regime, positive month-over-month growth in RSAFS reflects resilient consumer spending that supports near-term corporate revenue beats, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following week; in a high-inflation regime the same retail sales strength instead raises rate-hike fears and does not predict positive returns.
failure_condition: Positive RSAFS growth signal fails to produce statistically significant positive mean 5d forward returns (t-test p>0.05) within low-inflation subsample.
